'''Zutmore''' was a [[Snivvian/Legends|Snivvian]] known to cheat her family and lovers. When her deceitful ways were revealed, her family abandoned her on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. She could often be seen at [[Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina/Legends|Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina]] sitting in a stupor, reflecting on her past. She was also sometimes mistakenly called "Rachalt Hyst", but was still referred to as "Zutmore" by the [[Bartender/Legends|bartender]] [[Ackmena/Legends|Ackmena]]. Like all Snivvians, she was nicknamed "Snaggletooth" by other [[Species/Legends|species]].

{{Char-stub}}

==Behind the scenes==
In [[1978]], the [[Kenner Products|Kenner]] toy company created a figure of the [[Snivvian/Legends|Snivvian]], [[Zutton/Legends|Zutton]]. The first version wore a blue outfit and was the same size as a regular action figure. It was released with the Sears Cantina Adventure Playset. A second, revised version with a red outfit and shorter legs was released as an individually packaged action figure. The first version was labeled "Blue Snaggletooth" and became a very rare figure. The package of the second figure included a picture of Zutmore from the ''Holiday Special''. This seemed to imply that the Snivvian in ''The Star Wars Holiday Special'' was the same Snivvian, Zutton, that appeared in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]. However, Zutton has a different appearance than the Snivvian in the ''Holiday Special'', and it was decided to split the appearances into separate characters.<ref name="Star Wars Blog">{{SW|url=news/the-star-wars-holiday-special-cantina-we-may-not-thrive-friend-but-we-survive-friend|text=''The Star Wars Holiday Special'' Cantina: Who's Who|int=The Star Wars Holiday Special Cantina: Who's Who|nolive=1}}</ref>

To add to the confusion, in [[2001]], [[Hasbro]] produced a figure under the name "Zutton," but the figure actually resembles Zutmore.<ref name="rebelscum.com">{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: Power of the Jedi|pack=Zutton (Snaggletooth)|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/POTJzutton.asp}}</ref> Around the same time, [[Decipher]] identified Zutmore as "Rachalt Hyst" for its [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]] set, ''[[Tatooine Limited]]''. Later sources, including the article [[Rogues Gallery (department)|''Rogues Gallery: You're Barred!'']] in [[Star Wars Insider 144|''Star Wars Insider'' 144]], returned to the name from the ''Holiday Special'' script: "Zutmore."<ref name="Star Wars Blog" />
